summary: The macOS CI job failed during the build phase with 62 compilation errors, primarily from g++ and gcc invocations while building native modules and addons.
root_cause: C/C++ compilation failures in native modules during `make -C node build-ci` execution. Multiple g++ and gcc invocations failed, including addon builds in the test/addons directory.

evidence.reason: Multiple g++ compilation failures indicating native code build errors
Native addon build failures during test addon compilation phase
Large number of compilation errors reported during build
Build command initiated with make, followed by compilation failures

suggested_fix: Review the full g++ and gcc error output to identify specific compilation failures. Common causes on macOS include missing dependencies, incompatible compiler flags, or toolchain version mismatches. Check that xcode-select and required build tools are properly configured.
